subject: Geography
chapter: Solar Radiation, Heat Balance and Temperature

concepts.title: Solar Radiation is the primary source of energy for the Earth's climate system.
concepts.description: Solar radiation is the key driver of weather and climate patterns on Earth. It is the primary source of energy for the Earth's climate system, and its distribution varies greatly across the globe due to the Earth's spherical shape, tilt, and rotation.

concepts.title: Heat Balance and Temperature
concepts.description: Heat balance is the equilibrium state of the Earth's energy budget, where the amount of incoming solar radiation equals the amount of outgoing terrestrial radiation. Temperature is a measure of the average kinetic energy of particles in a substance, and it plays a crucial role in determining the Earth's climate.

concepts.title: Albedo Effect
concepts.description: The albedo effect refers to the reflection of solar radiation back into space by the Earth's surface, atmosphere, and clouds. Dark surfaces tend to absorb more solar radiation, while light surfaces tend to reflect it, affecting the Earth's temperature and climate.

concepts.title: SAD and WET
concepts.description: To remember the factors that affect the heat balance of the Earth, use the mnemonic SAD and WET: Solar Radiation, Atmospheric Gases, Deforestation, and Water Evaporation and Transpiration.

concepts.title: Confusing Heat and Temperature
concepts.description: Many students confuse heat and temperature, thinking they are the same thing. However, heat refers to the transfer of thermal energy, while temperature is a measure of the average kinetic energy of particles in a substance. Be sure to distinguish between the two in your exams.

concepts.title: SI Units for Temperature and Heat
concepts.description: To solve problems related to heat balance and temperature, remember the following SI units: Kelvin (K) for temperature, Joules (J) for heat, and Watts (W) for power.

concepts.title: Greenhouse Effect
concepts.description: The greenhouse effect is a natural process that occurs when certain gases in the Earth's atmosphere, such as carbon dioxide and methane, trap solar radiation and prevent it from escaping back into space.

concepts.title: Heat Budget and Energy Balance
concepts.description: The heat budget and energy balance of the Earth refer to the equilibrium state of the Earth's energy budget, where the amount of incoming solar radiation equals the amount of outgoing terrestrial radiation. This balance is crucial for maintaining the Earth's climate.
